name: biomedical-agent-teams
description: BMAT for Codex v1.2.0: a Codex-native biomedical workflow router with receipt-backed source verification, evidence-span claim gates, hash-bound bundles, independent-review provenance, domain packs, omics workflows, and experiment-design validation.

interface.shortDescription: BMAT for Codex 1.2.0: receipt-backed biomedical evidence, review, omics, experiment, and release validation.
interface.longDescription: BMAT for Codex v1.2.0 is a Codex-native biomedical workflow suite for structured life-science research. Its lightweight SKILL.md router lazy-loads six command recipes and explicit domain packs. Release bundles bind workflow identity, source-verification receipts, tool-call provenance, local evidence spans, claim-support scope, independent-review runtime receipts, results integration, and critical artifact hashes. The suite also retains Compact and Full tiers, 10x and bulk RNA-seq provenance contracts, public-omics metadata smoke checks, experiment-design gates, hypothesis-tournament checks, loop-state validation, migration of legacy bundles without promoting unknown evidence, adversarial golden cases, and validator findings with actionable fix_hint guidance. BMAT validates process and provenance; it does not certify scientific truth or replace human scientific review.
defaultPrompt: biomedical-research-council --domain-pack generic-biomedical audit evidence for a bounded biomedical claim
experiment-design-team --domain-pack generic-biomedical design a reproducible preclinical study
omics-analysis-team --tier full --track tenx-gex audit donor-aware single-cell provenance and pseudobulk
